projects.title: Unit 9 Project — Coordinate Plane & Integers in Action
projects.desc: Apply graphing, integers, and the coordinate plane to a real-world capstone challenge for Unit 9.

projects.title: Cartesian Odyssey
projects.desc: Plot and read points in a four-quadrant math sandbox (6.NS.6).

projects.title: Archipelago Coordinate Navigator
projects.desc: Plot ordered pairs on the coordinate plane in a pirate quest (6.NS.6).

projects.title: Treasure Map Navigator (Part 1)
projects.desc: Interactive graphic novel: graph points on the coordinate plane (6.NS.6).

title: Graph on the Coordinate Plane
contentObjective: I can plot and identify points on the coordinate plane using ordered pairs.
languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words coordinate plane, ordered pair, origin, and quadrant.

mission.title: Captain Vega's Coordinates
mission.story: You are the navigator on Captain Vega's expedition. Instead of 'X marks the spot,' she hid the treasure route as ordered pairs scrawled across a four-quadrant chart. Plot every point in the right order and the lost cove treasure is yours.
mission.objective: Plot ordered pairs accurately on the coordinate plane to trace the treasure route.

scenes.name: The Old Map
scenes.text: Decode Captain Vega's first clue: start at (2, 1), then (5, 4), then (8, 1).

scenes.name: Navigator's Log
scenes.text: Learn the chart's terms: coordinate plane, ordered pair, origin, quadrant, reflection, integer.

scenes.name: Chart Room
scenes.text: Plot the waypoints on the four-quadrant plane to reveal the route.

scenes.name: Course Plotting
scenes.text: Plot and read the points. The chart adapts the difficulty to keep you on course.

scenes.name: Sky Survey
scenes.text: Use ordered pairs to program a drone's delivery flight path.

scenes.name: Treasure Found
scenes.text: Mark the final spot and prove you can navigate by coordinates.

simulation.instructions: Plot Captain Vega's treasure waypoints on the four-quadrant plane. Remember: the first number moves you right/left, the second moves you up/down.

finale.title: Treasure Recovered!
finale.text: Every waypoint plotted perfectly, and the route led straight to the lost cove gold. Captain Vega just named you chief navigator — because you can plot any coordinate without missing a beat.

vocabulary.definition: A grid with a line going across and a line going up that cross.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una cuadrícula con una línea horizontal y una vertical que se cruzan.
vocabulary.visual: A grid with a horizontal line (x-axis) crossing a vertical line (y-axis), making four sections
vocabulary.cloze: The grid made by a horizontal and a vertical number line is the ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Mặt phẳng được tạo ra bởi hai trục vuông góc nhau tại gốc tọa độ.
vocabulary.definitionAr: مستوى يتكون من محورين متعامدين يلتقيان عند نقطة الأصل.

vocabulary.definition: Two numbers (x, y) that tell where a point is on a grid.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Dos números (x, y) que dicen dónde está un punto en una cuadrícula.
vocabulary.visual: (3, 5) means move right 3 from the origin, then up 5
vocabulary.cloze: Two numbers (x, y) that tell the location of a point are an ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Cặp số chỉ vị trí một điểm trên mặt phẳng tọa độ (x, y).
vocabulary.definitionAr: زوج من الأعداد يحدد موقع نقطة على المستوى الإحداثي (x, y).

vocabulary.definition: The point (0, 0) where the two grid lines cross.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El punto (0, 0) donde se cruzan las dos líneas de la cuadrícula.
vocabulary.visual: The starting point at the center where both axes cross — (0, 0)
vocabulary.cloze: The point (0, 0) where the x-axis and y-axis cross is the ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Điểm giao nhau của hai trục tọa độ, có tọa độ là (0, 0).
vocabulary.definitionAr: نقطة تقاطع المحورين في المستوى الإحداثي وإحداثياتها (0, 0).

vocabulary.definition: One of the four parts of a coordinate grid.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una de las cuatro partes de una cuadrícula de coordenadas.
vocabulary.visual: I (+,+) top-right, II (-,+) top-left, III (-,-) bottom-left, IV (+,-) bottom-right
vocabulary.cloze: One of the four regions of the coordinate plane is a ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Một trong bốn phần của mặt phẳng tọa độ chia bởi hai trục.
vocabulary.definitionAr: أحد الأجزاء الأربعة للمستوى الإحداثي الناتجة عن تقاطع المحورين.

vocabulary.definition: A flipped, mirror image across a line.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una imagen reflejada al otro lado de una línea.
vocabulary.visual: (3, 2) reflected over the x-axis becomes (3, -2) — same x, opposite y
vocabulary.cloze: A flip of a point or shape over an axis is a ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Phép lật hình hoặc điểm qua một đường thẳng (trục đối xứng).
vocabulary.definitionAr: قلب شكل أو نقطة فوق خط مستقيم (محور الانعكاس).

vocabulary.definition: Whole numbers and their opposites, like -2, -1, 0, 1, 2.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Números enteros y sus opuestos, como -2, -1, 0, 1, 2.
vocabulary.cloze: A whole number or its opposite, with no fraction part, is an ___.

examples.why: It is a whole number's opposite with no fraction.
It has a fraction part, so it is not an integer.

vocabulary.definitionVi: Tập hợp các số nguyên dương, nguyên âm và số không.
vocabulary.definitionAr: مجموعة الأعداد الكاملة الموجبة والسالبة والصفر.

launch.narrative: Captain Vega left a treasure map, but instead of an 'X marks the spot,' she hid the directions as ordered pairs! To find the treasure, you need to plot each point on the coordinate plane and connect them in order. The first clue says: Start at (2, 1), then go to (5, 4), then (8, 1).
launch.contextImage: Old parchment treasure map with a coordinate grid overlay and compass rose
noticePrompts: What shape will the three points make when connected?
Which number tells you how far right/left? Which tells you up/down?
Why is the ORDER in an ordered pair important?
wonderPrompts: What if one of the coordinates was negative?
How would you describe a location without a coordinate plane?

conceptIntro.heading: How do you plot a point on the coordinate plane?
conceptIntro.intro: An ordered pair (x, y) is two numbers that tell you exactly where a point goes. The first number moves you right or left. The second number moves you up or down.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: I will plot the point (5, 4). The 5 is the x-coordinate, and the 4 is the y-coordinate.
I start at the origin, which is (0, 0).
First I move 5 units to the right along the x-axis.
Then I move 4 units up. I place my dot. That is the point (5, 4).

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Now let's plot (2, 1). Where do we start? At the origin (0, 0).
How far do we move right? The first number is 2, so we move 2 right.
How far do we move up? The second number is 1, so we move 1 up and place the dot.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the explore, plot Captain Vega's waypoints (2, 1), (5, 4), and (8, 1) to trace the treasure route.

explore.instructions: Plot Captain Vega's treasure map points and connect them to reveal the path!
explore.label: Plot the three treasure map waypoints.

discourse.prompt: You plotted a triangle. How did the ordered pairs tell you exactly where to place each point?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The first number in the ordered pair tells me ___ and the second number tells me ___. The point (5, 4) means ___.

approaching.stem: Which ordered pair names a point 3 units right and 7 units up from the origin?
approaching.explanation: The first coordinate is horizontal (right 3), the second is vertical (up 7). So (3, 7).

approaching.stem: What are the coordinates of the origin?
approaching.explanation: The origin is where the x-axis and y-axis cross, at (0, 0).

approaching.stem: A point is at (6, 2). What does the 6 tell you?
approaching.explanation: The first number (x-coordinate) tells you horizontal movement. 6 means go right 6 units from the origin.

approaching.label: Put these steps in order for plotting the point (4, 5).
items: Move 4 units to the right along the x-axis
Move 5 units up from that spot
Place a dot at the point (4, 5)
correctOrder: Move 4 units to the right along the x-axis
Move 5 units up from that spot
Place a dot at the point (4, 5)

approaching.label: Match each ordered pair to its description.

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Graph on the Coordinate Plane.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.

onLevel.label: Plot these points to reveal a hidden shape: (1, 1), (1, 5), (6, 5), (6, 1)
Match each point to its quadrant or axis.

onLevel.instructions: Complete the table. Write the ordered pair for each description.

onLevel.label: Sort these ordered pairs by their x-coordinate from smallest to largest.

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Graph on the Coordinate Plane.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.

extending.title: Find the Plotting Error
extending.correctWork: x-coordinate = horizontal (right 4), NOT vertical. The student swapped x and y directions.
hints: In an ordered pair (x, y), which direction does x go — horizontal or vertical?
x is always horizontal (left/right) first. y is vertical (up/down) second. The student went up first.

extending.prompt: A student says that (3, 5) and (5, 3) are the same point because they use the same numbers. Is the student correct? Explain why or why not, and describe where each point is located on the coordinate plane.
extending.sentenceFrame: The student is ___ because (3, 5) means ___ while (5, 3) means ___. The ORDER matters because ___.

extending.label: Plot the points (2, 3), (2, 7), (6, 7), (6, 3). Connect them. Name the shape and find its perimeter using the coordinates.

optionalActivity.name: Treasure Map Navigator Quest
optionalActivity.intro: Follow Captain Vega's coordinate clues across the four-quadrant chart to find the buried treasure.

optional.stem: Stage 1 — Captain Vega's note says the first landmark, Palm Cove, is 6 units to the right and 4 units up from the origin. Which ordered pair names Palm Cove?
optional.explanation: The first number is horizontal (right 6) and the second is vertical (up 4), so Palm Cove is at (6, 4).

optional.stem: Stage 2 — The next clue points to Skull Rock at (-5, 3). Which quadrant is Skull Rock in?
optional.explanation: The x-coordinate is negative (left) and the y-coordinate is positive (up). Negative x and positive y land in the top-left region, Quadrant II.

optional.instructions: Stage 3 — Sort each treasure landmark into the correct quadrant based on its ordered pair (x, y).

optional.label: Stage 4 — Match each landmark on the map to its ordered pair.

optional.stem: Stage 5 — Captain Vega marks the final treasure 0 units across and 8 units up from the origin. Where does this point land?
optional.explanation: An x-coordinate of 0 means no horizontal move, so the point stays on the y-axis. Moving up 8 places it at (0, 8).

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Fix the Swapped Coordinates

workedExample.label: Plot the point
workedExample.work: Move right 5, then up 2, and place the dot at (5, 2).

optional.correctWork: The student swapped x and y. For (2, 5), move right 2 first (the x-coordinate), then up 5 (the y-coordinate), and place the dot at (2, 5). Plotting at (5, 2) is a different spot.
hints: In an ordered pair (x, y), the first number is always horizontal and the second is always vertical.
Compare (2, 5) and (5, 2): (2, 5) is right 2 and up 5, while (5, 2) is right 5 and up 2 — they are different locations on the map.

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in Graph on the Coordinate Plane is skipping the key idea: "Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: A drone delivery company programs flight paths using coordinate pairs. A drone starts at the warehouse (0, 0), flies to pickup at (3, 6), then delivers to (8, 2), then returns to base (0, 0).
connect.promptQuestion: How do ordered pairs help the drone navigate? What shape does the flight path make?
connect.prompt: The drone uses ordered pairs because the first number controls ___ movement and the second controls ___. The path makes a ___ shape.

exitTicket.stem: Point P is located 6 units right and 3 units up from the origin. What ordered pair names point P?
exitTicket.explanation: Right 6 = x-coordinate of 6, Up 3 = y-coordinate of 3. The ordered pair is (6, 3).

turnAndTalk.question: On a treasure map laid over a coordinate plane, why do we always start at the origin (0, 0) and move sideways before moving up or down to reach the treasure?
turnAndTalk.kernel: We start at the origin and move along the x-axis first because ___.

stems.en: The first number in (x, y) tells me to move ___.
stems.es: El primer numero en (x, y) me dice que me mueva ___.

stems.en: The second number tells me to move ___.
stems.es: El segundo numero me dice que me mueva ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student states the x-coordinate is the sideways move and the y-coordinate is the up/down move, both starting from the origin.
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to explain why the treasure spots (3, 5) and (5, 3) are different locations even though they use the same numbers.
extendStems: The order in an ordered pair matters because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Your map says the treasure is at (4, 2). Walk your partner through every move you make from the origin, using the words x-axis and y-axis.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Starting at the origin, I first move ___, then I move ___.

stems.en: I move ___ units along the x-axis.
stems.es: Me muevo ___ unidades por el eje x.

stems.en: Then I move ___ units along the y-axis.
stems.es: Luego me muevo ___ unidades por el eje y.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student moves 4 right along the x-axis and 2 up along the y-axis, naming each axis and the origin as the start.
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to predict which quadrant the treasure at (4, 2) lands in and justify using the signs of the coordinates.
extendStems: The treasure at (4, 2) lands in quadrant ___ because ___.
I can tell the quadrant from the coordinates by ___.

turnAndTalk.question: How is reading a coordinate pair on the treasure map like reading directions in a video game or on a real map?
turnAndTalk.kernel: An ordered pair works like map directions because ___.

stems.es: La coordenada x es como ir ___.
La coordenada y es como ir ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student connects the x-value to horizontal travel and the y-value to vertical travel, like map directions from a start point.
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to generalize a rule for plotting ANY treasure location, including ones with a zero coordinate.
extendStems: A rule that always works for plotting (x, y) is ___.
If one coordinate is 0, the treasure lands on ___ because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A teammate marked the treasure at (2, 6) by moving up 2 and over 6. What mistake did they make, and how would you coach them?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The mistake was ___, and to fix it they should ___.

stems.en: They mixed up the ___ and the ___.
The correct way is to move ___ first.
stems.es: La forma correcta es moverse ___ primero.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student identifies that the x- and y-coordinates were swapped and explains the correct order (x first, then y).
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to critique why this swap error is so common on maps and suggest a strategy to avoid it.
extendStems: This error happens a lot because ___.
A strategy to remember the order is ___.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on Graph on the Coordinate Plane, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.

stems.en: I move ___ units along the x-axis.
stems.es: Me muevo ___ unidades por el eje x.

stems.en: I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.NS.6 — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

learningTonight.en: Plot ordered pairs accurately on the coordinate plane to trace the treasure route.
learningTonight.es: Practicar: Graph on the Coordinate Plane

conceptSteps.en: You are the navigator on Captain Vega's expedition.
conceptSteps.es: Lean la historia de la misión juntos antes de empezar los ejercicios.

conceptSteps.en: Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.
conceptSteps.es: Verifiquen cada paso con la idea clave de la lección.

watchFor.en: Listen for "Coordinate plane" — A grid with a line going across and a line going up that cross.
watchFor.es: Escucha "Plano cartesiano" — Una cuadrícula con una línea horizontal y una vertical que se cruzan.

watchFor.en: Listen for "Ordered pair" — Two numbers (x, y) that tell where a point is on a grid.
watchFor.es: Escucha "Par ordenado" — Dos números (x, y) que dicen dónde está un punto en una cuadrícula.

watchFor.en: Listen for "Origin" — The point (0, 0) where the two grid lines cross.
watchFor.es: Escucha "Origen" — El punto (0, 0) donde se cruzan las dos líneas de la cuadrícula.

tryTogether.scenarioEn: You are the navigator on Captain Vega's expedition. Instead of 'X marks the spot,' she hid the treasure route as ordered pairs scrawled across a four-quadrant chart. Plot every point in the right order and the lost cove treasure is yours.
tryTogether.scenarioEs: Usen la historia de la misión para conectar la matemática con un problema real.

steps.en: Pick one practice problem and solve it on paper together.
steps.es: Elijan un problema de práctica y resuélvanlo en papel juntos.
steps.hintEs: Hablen de cada paso en voz alta.

steps.en: Check: does your answer follow — Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.?
steps.es: Verifiquen: ¿su respuesta sigue — Always start at the origin (0, 0), move right/left first, then up/down.?
steps.hint: This is the flagship lesson's deepest idea.
steps.hintEs: Esta es la idea más profunda de la lección estrella.

say.en: What part of the mission story helps here?
say.es: ¿Qué parte de la historia de la misión ayuda aquí?

say.en: Let's re-read the key idea together.

dontSay.en: This flagship lesson is too hard — skip it.
dontSay.es: Esta lección estrella es muy difícil — sáltenla.

printables.name: Coordinate Map Mission
printables.desc: Teams design coordinate treasure maps, write ordered-pair clues, and verify a partner team’s route.

printables.name: Graph on the Coordinate Plane — Color by Number
printables.desc: Solve each problem, then color by the answer to reveal a picture.

printables.name: Graph on the Coordinate Plane — Vocabulary Word Search
printables.desc: Find this lesson's key vocabulary terms in the puzzle.

printables.name: Graph on the Coordinate Plane — MCAP-Style Practice
printables.desc: MCAP-style selected- and constructed-response practice items.

graphicNovel.title: Axiom City: The Grid Map
graphicNovel.desc: Interactive Axiom City episode — read straight through; gates coach but never block. Not graded.
